query_normalized: investigating neural signatures of predictive processing in visual illusions neuroscience
rationale: The paper focuses on deep learning architecture for medical image segmentation (a computer vision/engineering task) and does not measure neural signatures, predictive processing mechanisms, or visual illusions in a biological or cognitive neuroscience context. It falls under the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule as it addresses a different scientific domain (AI application vs. cognitive neuroscience) despite sharing the keyword "neural" in the context of artificial networks.
